free_text: Activation cross-sections of deuteron induced nuclear
reactions on gold up to 40 MeV
High purity Au foils from Goodfellow
Deuteron beams of 40 and 20 MeV were used.
Target and monitor foils were irradiated in
Measurements started after 1.5 h cooling time for
20 MeV irradiation and 1 day cooling time of 40 MeV
The beam intensities obtained via charge collection
were corrected with the data from the monitor
The energy degradation was initially determined by
calculation using the formulas and tables of Andersen
and Ziegler and corrected on the basis of the
The uncertainty on the primary incident
energy is estimated to be 0.3 MeV while due to
cumulative effects the uncertainty increases to
1.2 MeV on the last foils of the stack.
The estimated total uncertainty on the
cross-sections was about 9.3 - 16.4 % by taking the
square root of the sum in quadrature of individual
No individual error components are given.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
f=0.914 is the decay branching ratio of m to g.
